verses.text: Zacchaeus, a rich chief publican, short of stature, climbs a tree to see Jesus. Eager to see. He overcomes both shame and the crowd to glimpse the Lord.
Zacchaeus, a rich chief publican, short of stature, climbs a tree to see Jesus. Eager to see. He overcomes both shame and the crowd to glimpse the Lord.
Zacchaeus, a rich chief publican, short of stature, climbs a tree to see Jesus. Eager to see. He overcomes both shame and the crowd to glimpse the Lord.
Zacchaeus, a rich chief publican, short of stature, climbs a tree to see Jesus. Eager to see. He overcomes both shame and the crowd to glimpse the Lord.
Jesus calls him down, 'to day I must abide at thy house'; the people murmur, He is gone to a sinner. Sought grace. Christ seeks the one the crowd despises.
Jesus calls him down, 'to day I must abide at thy house'; the people murmur, He is gone to a sinner. Sought grace. Christ seeks the one the crowd despises.
Jesus calls him down, 'to day I must abide at thy house'; the people murmur, He is gone to a sinner. Sought grace. Christ seeks the one the crowd despises.
Zacchaeus gives half his goods and restores fourfold; 'the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.' Changed life. True faith bears fruit in restitution and generosity.

Zacchaeus gives half his goods and restores fourfold; 'the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.' Changed life. True faith bears fruit in restitution and generosity.
A nobleman gives ten servants pounds to trade, while citizens reject his reign. A trust given. The Lord entrusts His own and goes to receive His kingdom.
A nobleman gives ten servants pounds to trade, while citizens reject his reign. A trust given. The Lord entrusts His own and goes to receive His kingdom.
A nobleman gives ten servants pounds to trade, while citizens reject his reign. A trust given. The Lord entrusts His own and goes to receive His kingdom.
A nobleman gives ten servants pounds to trade, while citizens reject his reign. A trust given. The Lord entrusts His own and goes to receive His kingdom.
On reckoning, the faithful who gained ten and five are set over cities. Reward of diligence. Faithful use of God's gifts brings increasing trust.
On reckoning, the faithful who gained ten and five are set over cities. Reward of diligence. Faithful use of God's gifts brings increasing trust.

One hides his pound in a napkin, blaming the lord as austere; 'out of thine own mouth' he is judged. Sloth condemned. The excuse only proves the servant's guilt.

His pound is given to him with ten; 'unto every one which hath shall be given.' Use it or lose it. The unused gift is taken away.
His pound is given to him with ten; 'unto every one which hath shall be given.' Use it or lose it. The unused gift is taken away.
His pound is given to him with ten; 'unto every one which hath shall be given.' Use it or lose it. The unused gift is taken away.
The enemies who would not have him reign are slain; He goes up to Jerusalem. Reign rejected. To refuse Christ's rule is to invite ruin.
The enemies who would not have him reign are slain; He goes up to Jerusalem. Reign rejected. To refuse Christ's rule is to invite ruin.
He sends for a colt; 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
He sends for a colt; 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
He sends for a colt; 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
They find the colt as He said and set Jesus on it. Exact word. His foreknowledge proves true in every detail.
They find the colt as He said and set Jesus on it. Exact word. His foreknowledge proves true in every detail.
They find the colt as He said and set Jesus on it. Exact word. His foreknowledge proves true in every detail.
They find the colt as He said and set Jesus on it. Exact word. His foreknowledge proves true in every detail.
They spread garments; the disciples praise God, 'Blessed be the King... peace in heaven.' Glad welcome. Heaven and earth rejoice in the coming King.
They spread garments; the disciples praise God, 'Blessed be the King... peace in heaven.' Glad welcome. Heaven and earth rejoice in the coming King.
They spread garments; the disciples praise God, 'Blessed be the King... peace in heaven.' Glad welcome. Heaven and earth rejoice in the coming King.
The Pharisees would silence them; 'if these should hold their peace, the stones would... cry out.' Praise unstoppable. Christ's glory will be confessed, by men or by stones.
The Pharisees would silence them; 'if these should hold their peace, the stones would... cry out.' Praise unstoppable. Christ's glory will be confessed, by men or by stones.

He weeps over the city, 'if thou hadst known... the things which belong unto thy peace'; thou knewest not 'the time of thy visitation.' Tears of love. He grieves for the city that rejects its day of grace.

He casts out the temple traders, 'My house is the house of prayer'; the priests seek to destroy Him. Holy zeal. He cleanses His Father's house from greed.
